import CategoryTheoryInContextLean.Section_1_1

/-!
# Category Theory in Context - Section 1.2

We introduce the opposite category, monomorphisms and epimorphisms.
Also we define sections, retractions, retracts, split monos and split epis.
We continue to use the custom definition of a category from Section 1.1.
-/

namespace CategoryInContext

open Category

-- need a type synomym the types in the opposite category
-- so that typeclass resolution doesn't get confused
-- by having two instances of Category on the same type.
def Opposite (╬▒ : Type*) := ╬▒

-- definition 1.2.1
instance Category.opp {╬▒ : Type*} [C : Category ╬▒] : Category (Opposite ╬▒) where
  Hom X Y := C.Hom Y X
  id X := C.id X
  comp f g := C.comp g f
  id_comp := by simp [comp_id]
  comp_id := by simp [id_comp]
  assoc := by simp [ÔćÉ assoc]

-- exmaple 1.2.2.i
def Category.MatR' (╬▒ : Type*) [Ring ╬▒] : Category ÔäĽ where
  Hom m n := Matrix (Fin m) (Fin n) ╬▒
  id := 1
  comp f g := f * g
  id_comp := by simp
  comp_id := by simp
  assoc _ _ _ := by rw [Matrix.mul_assoc]

variable {╬▒ : Type*} [C : Category ╬▒]

theorem Category.MatR_opp_eq_MatR' [Ring ╬▒] : (MatR ╬▒).opp = MatR' ╬▒ := by sorry

-- exmaple 1.2.2.ii
noncomputable instance Category.Poset' (╬▒ : Type*) [PartialOrder ╬▒] : Category ╬▒ where
  Hom X Y := PLift (Y ÔëĄ X) -- some universe gymnastics to move between Prop and Type
  id X := ÔčĘle_refl XÔčę
  comp f g := ÔčĘle_trans g.down f.downÔčę
  id_comp _ :=

import Mathlib

/-!
# Category Theory in Context - Section 1.2

We introduce the opposite category, monomorphisms and epimorphisms.
Also we define sections, retractions, retracts, split monos and split epis.
We continue to use the custom definition of a category from Section 1.1.
-/

namespace CategoryInContext

open Category

-- need a type synomym the types in the opposite category
-- so that typeclass resolution doesn't get confused
-- by having two instances of Category on the same type.
def Opposite (╬▒ : Type*) := ╬▒

-- definition 1.2.1
instance Category.opp {╬▒ : Type*} [C : Category ╬▒] : Category (Opposite ╬▒) where
  Hom X Y := C.Hom Y X
  id X := C.id X
  comp f g := C.comp g f
  id_comp := by simp [comp_id]
  comp_id := by simp [id_comp]
  assoc := by simp [ÔćÉ assoc]

-- exmaple 1.2.2.i
def Category.MatR' (╬▒ : Type*) [Ring ╬▒] : Category ÔäĽ where
  Hom m n := Matrix (Fin m) (Fin n) ╬▒
  id := 1
  comp f g := f * g
  id_comp := by simp
  comp_id := by simp
  assoc _ _ _ := by rw [Matrix.mul_assoc]

variable {╬▒ : Type*} [C : Category ╬▒]

theorem Category.MatR_opp_eq_MatR' [Ring ╬▒] : (MatR ╬▒).opp = MatR' ╬▒ := by sorry

-- exmaple 1.2.2.ii
noncomputable instance Category.Poset' (╬▒ : Type*) [PartialOrder ╬▒] : Category ╬▒ where
  Hom X Y := PLift (Y ÔëĄ X) -- some universe gymnastics to move between Prop and Type
  id X := ÔčĘle_refl XÔčę
  comp f g := ÔčĘle_trans g.down f.downÔčę
  id_comp _ := rfl
  comp_id _ := rfl
  assoc _ _ _ := rfl

theorem Category.Poset_opp_eq_Poset' [PartialOrder ╬▒] : (Poset ╬▒).opp = Poset' ╬▒ := by sorry

-- todo add 1.2.2.iii

  [ IsIso f,
    ÔłÇ c : ╬▒, Function.Bijective (fun g : Hom c X => g Ôëź f),
    ÔłÇ c : ╬▒, Function.Bijective (fun g : Hom Y c => f Ôëź g)
  ].TFAE := by sorry

-- definition 1.2.7
def Category.Mono {X Y : ╬▒} (f : Hom X Y) : Prop :=
  ÔłÇ {W} (h k : Hom W X), h Ôëź f = k Ôëź f Ôćĺ h = k
def Category.Epi {X Y : ╬▒} (f : Hom X Y) : Prop :=
  ÔłÇ {Z} (h k : Hom Y Z), f Ôëź h = f Ôëź k Ôćĺ h = k

lemma Category.mono_op_epi {X Y : ╬▒} (f : Hom X Y) :
    Mono f Ôćö @Epi (Opposite ╬▒) _ Y X f := by
  sorry

lemma Category.epi_op_mono {X Y : ╬▒} (f : Hom X Y) :
    Epi f Ôćö @Mono (Opposite ╬▒) _ Y X f := by
  sorry

-- todo: add support for custom notation: X Ôćĺ Y for Hom X Y and X Ôćú Y for {f: Hom X Y // Epi f}

theorem Category.Mono_injection {X Y : ╬▒} (f : Hom X Y) (hf : Mono f) :
    ÔłÇ c : ╬▒, Function.Injective (fun g : Hom c X => g Ôëź f) := by

theorem Category.Epi_surjection {X Y : ╬▒} (f : Hom X Y) (hf : Epi f) :
    ÔłÇ c : ╬▒, Function.Injective (fun g : Hom Y c => f Ôëź g) := by
